expected_properties: c reflects a - b for each successive input pair without spurious glitches
additional_cases.goal: Verify that rapid successive changes on a and b produce correct combinational outputs each evaluation cycle
notes: No internal state is assumed; the module is combinational per contract
preconditions: Inputs a and b are driven with legal N‑bit values each cycle
stimulus_intent: Apply a sequence of diverse legal patterns on a and b with no idle cycles
timing_assumptions: Output c is expected to settle after input changes within the same combinational evaluation window

expected_properties: c becomes X when any input contains X or Z, reflecting unknown propagation
additional_cases.goal: Confirm that illegal/unknown input values propagate to the output as undefined
notes: Contract does not forbid X/Z, so this tests conservative unknown handling
preconditions: Drive a or b with X/Z values
stimulus_intent: Introduce X/Z on a or b while keeping the other input legal
timing_assumptions: Observe c after inputs settle; no timing guarantee required

expected_properties: c equals the unsigned subtraction a - b modulo 2^N
baseline_case_enrichments.goal: Validate basic combinational subtraction across typical legal values
notes: Ensures functional mapping without relying on hidden state or latency
stimulus_intent: Apply random legal N‑bit patterns to inputs a and b
timing_assumptions: Outputs are observed after inputs have stabilized; combinational behavior only

expected_properties: c correctly reflects subtraction at extreme values (all zeros, all ones, one‑hot patterns) without overflow artifacts
baseline_case_enrichments.goal: Exercise boundary conditions and width‑sensitive scenarios
notes: Covers potential edge effects in unsigned subtraction for the full N‑bit range
stimulus_intent: Drive min, max, and one‑hot patterns on a and b
timing_assumptions: Observe after inputs settle; no fixed latency assumed
